title: HITL Resume & Approval Flow
interaction_flow: Runner → run_single_turn: get model response + call execute_tools_and_side_effects()
Runner → Caller: return RunResult with .interruptions populated
Caller → RunState: state.approve(interruption, always_approve=False) OR state.reject(interruption, rejection_message=...)
Caller (optional) → RunState: state.to_string() / to_json() — serialize for durable storage
Caller (optional) → RunState: RunState.from_json(agent, stored) — deserialize from storage
Runner: detect run_state._current_step is NextStepInterruption → 'Continuing from interruption'
Runner → resolve_interrupted_turn(bindings, original_input, generated_items, last_model_response, last_processed_response, run_state=run_state)
resolve_interrupted_turn → _select_function_tool_runs_for_resume(processed_response.functions, ...): filter which function runs to re-execute
→ output_exists_checker(run): if output already in pre_step_items → skip (already done)
→ if approval_status is False: record_rejection(call_id, tool_call, function_tool) → append rejection error item
→ if approval_status is True: add to selected (approved runs)
→ if not requires_approval: add to selected (no approval needed)
→ if approval_status is None: pending_interruption_adder(pending_item_builder(run)) → still blocked
resolve_interrupted_turn → _collect_runs_by_approval(shell_calls, ...): same filter logic for ShellTool runs
resolve_interrupted_turn → _collect_runs_by_approval(apply_patch_calls, ...): same filter for ApplyPatchTool runs
resolve_interrupted_turn → _collect_runs_by_approval(custom_tool_calls, ...): same filter for custom tool runs

resolve_interrupted_turn → _build_plan_for_resume_turn(processed_response, function_runs=function_tool_runs, shell_calls=approved_shell, apply_patch_calls=approved_apply_patch, custom_tool_calls=approved_custom, pending_interruptions=..., ...): build ToolExecutionPlan
resolve_interrupted_turn → _execute_tool_plan(plan, bindings, hooks, context_wrapper, run_config): execute approved tools in parallel
_execute_tool_plan → execute_function_tool_calls / execute_shell_calls / execute_apply_patch_calls / execute_custom_tool_calls: run approved tools
resolve_interrupted_turn → _collect_tool_interruptions(function_results, custom_tool_results, ...): collect any NEW nested interruptions
resolve_interrupted_turn → _build_tool_result_items: assemble new_step_items from all results
Runner → resumed_turn_items(turn_result): split into generated_items + session_items
Runner → save_resumed_turn_items(session, items=turn_session_items, persisted_count, response_id): persist to session store
save_resumed_turn_items → Session.add_items(items): write to session backend
Runner (if next_step still NextStepInterruption): save RunResult with interruptions → return to Caller again
Runner (if next_step resolved): is_resumed_state = False → continue normal turn loop → final output
key_relationships: Runner orchestrates the full resume lifecycle — detects NextStepInterruption state and delegates to resolve_interrupted_turn
resolve_interrupted_turn reads approval decisions via context_wrapper.get_approval_status(tool_name, call_id)
_select_function_tool_runs_for_resume uses approval_items_by_call_id + output_index to skip already-done/rejected calls
_build_plan_for_resume_turn composes ToolExecutionPlan from pre-filtered approved run lists
append_approval_error_output emits synthetic ToolCallOutputItem for rejected calls (model sees why tool was blocked)
update_run_state_after_resume mutates RunState in place after resume: sets _original_input, _generated_items, _session_items, _current_step
save_resumed_turn_items persists new tool outputs to Session store (skips already-persisted items via persisted_count)
Partial approval supported: unresolved interruptions re-surface on next run, resolved ones execute normally

key_data_structures.ToolApprovalItem: agent, raw_item (tool call), tool_name, tool_namespace, tool_origin, tool_lookup_key
key_data_structures.NextStepInterruption: interruptions: list[ToolApprovalItem] — signals run is suspended
key_data_structures.RunState._current_step: NextStepInterruption when suspended; cleared after resume completes
key_data_structures.RunState._approvals (via RunContextWrapp: dict[tool_name, ApprovalRecord(approved: bool|set, rejected: bool|set, rejection_message?)]
key_data_structures.ToolExecutionPlan: function_runs, computer_actions, custom_tool_calls, shell_calls, apply_patch_calls, local_shell_calls, pending_interruptions, approved_mcp_responses, mcp_requests_with_callback
key_data_structures.SingleStepResult: original_input, model_response, pre_step_items, new_step_items, next_step, tool_guardrail_results, processed_response
key_data_structures.approval_items_by_call_id: dict[call_id, ToolApprovalItem] — fast lookup of pending approvals

important_notes: Resume does NOT re-query the LLM — it replays the last processed_response with resolved approvals
already-executed outputs are skipped via _build_tool_output_index (avoids double-execution)
always_approve=True / always_reject=True persist decisions for ALL future calls to that tool in the same run (sticky decisions survive serialization)
rejection_message per call overrides run-wide RunConfig.tool_error_formatter
Nested Agent.as_tool() interruptions surface on the outer run's interruptions list — outer RunState handles them, not the nested runner
Streaming resume: same flow via start_streaming(is_resumed_state=True) → resolves interruptions then streams new output
Session integration: save_resumed_turn_items uses persisted_count to avoid re-persisting already saved items after retries
